quotes.quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.
quotes.rationale: Identifies an LLM evaluator whose output is a ranking over stated quality criteria, selecting which candidates proceed to experimental evaluation.

quotes.quote: Given a disease, Robin automatically identifies relevant in vitro assays that model key disease mechanisms and proposes specific drug candidates to evaluate in these experimental models.
quotes.rationale: Establishes that the ranked objects are automatically generated candidates passed forward to an experimental stage.

quotes.quote: When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.
quotes.rationale: A human-expert comparison and repeated-comparison consistency study of the LLM judge; an agreement/consistency design reported as a diagnostic, not a gate, and not a probabilistic calibration analysis.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.
quotes.rationale: Physical experiments are executed by human researchers, not by the system; the resulting empirical data is the evidence entering the loop.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
quotes.rationale: Shows the experimental outcome is routed back into generation of a new candidate round, i.e. candidate revision rather than terminal assessment.

quotes.quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
quotes.rationale: Delimits the automated analysis component: upstream demultiplexing/alignment was human-performed, differential expression analysis was performed by the component Finch.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
quotes.rationale: The analysis output is interpreted to produce a new round of candidates, establishing candidate-revision routing for this analysis channel.

quotes.quote: Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.
quotes.rationale: A distinct human-performed analysis channel over assay data; the packet does not state where this analysis output is routed.

quotes.quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.
quotes.rationale: The source states executability of generated protocols is not achieved and is a future aim, so no executability check is exercised in this version.

policy_type: LLM-driven candidate and assay generation from a disease input
LLM-judged tournament ranking over stated criteria to select candidates for experimental evaluation
iterative human-in-the-loop cycle in which supplied experimental data drives a new candidate round

diagnostics.statement: The candidate-selection gate is an LLM-judged tournament over rationale, pharmacological profile, and literature methodology; no non-LLM or independent evidence source constrains which candidates advance to experiment.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.

diagnostics.consequence: Systematic preferences of the single LLM judge propagate unchecked into which candidates consume physical assay resources.

diagnostics.statement: The reported LLM-judge evaluation is an expert-agreement and repeated-comparison consistency study; it establishes concordance and self-consistency but not probabilistic calibration or property-specific external validation, and the human reference itself agreed with its own prior choices only 61% of the time.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.

diagnostics.consequence: Agreement with a noisy human reference cannot support claims that judge scores track real-world candidate quality, so reliability for the downstream gate remains not established.

diagnostics.statement: Physical experiments are executed by human researchers who then supply the data back to the system; the loop closes only through human execution and data provision.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.
Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.

diagnostics.consequence: The boundary between autonomous computational feedback and human-mediated experimental execution is not sharply drawn, making autonomy claims hard to assess.

diagnostics.statement: The source states the system does not yet produce precise, executable protocols, so no executability validation of generated experimental outlines is exercised.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols

diagnostics.consequence: Human translation is required between generated outlines and laboratory execution, inserting an unvalidated interpretive step into the experimental chain.

diagnostics.statement: Key analysis steps were performed by humans rather than the automated component: read demultiplexing and alignment, and the flow cytometry analysis.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

diagnostics.consequence: The share of analysis attributable to the automated validator is narrower than a whole-workflow reading would suggest, so autonomy must be coded stepwise.

diagnostics.statement: No independent replication of the reported experimental findings by a separate laboratory is reported anywhere in the packet.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.

diagnostics.consequence: The empirical evidence entering candidate revision rests on a single execution setting, leaving reproducibility unestablished.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.
the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10

success_check.observable: Assay outcome for candidates sampled across the full LLM tournament ranking, including low-ranked candidates
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Top-ranked candidates show a significantly higher assay hit rate than low-ranked candidates tested under the same protocol
success_check.evidence_needed: Pre-registered rank-stratified physical assay results linking judge rank to measured experimental outcome

exact_quotes.exact_quote: When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons

success_check.observable: Change in judge preference under controlled perturbations of candidate presentation (order, phrasing, citation density)
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: No perturbation produces a preference-flip rate materially exceeding the judge's measured 12% self-inconsistency
success_check.evidence_needed: A systematic bias test over matched candidate pairs reporting perturbation-wise flip rates

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.
Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

success_check.observable: A published step-by-step attribution table assigning each generation, execution, analysis, and decision step to human or automated component
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Every reported workflow step carries an explicit actor attribution with no unattributed steps
success_check.evidence_needed: Workflow attribution documentation accompanying the reported runs

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.

success_check.observable: Direction and effect size of the key assay readouts as measured by a laboratory not involved in the original runs
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Independent laboratory reproduces the reported effect direction with overlapping confidence intervals
success_check.evidence_needed: Replication report with raw measurements from the independent laboratory

exact_quotes.exact_quote: future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution

success_check.observable: Documented human review and sign-off of each generated experimental outline before laboratory execution
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: All executed outlines carry a recorded reviewer approval and an enumerated list of translation edits
success_check.evidence_needed: Protocol review logs paired with executed laboratory records

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament
the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B)

success_check.observable: Reporting that distinguishes the judge ranking used as an advancement gate from the expert-comparison study used only as a diagnostic
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Gate criteria and diagnostic reliability metrics documented as separate artifacts with distinct thresholds
success_check.evidence_needed: System documentation specifying which evaluator outputs alter candidate advancement and which are reported only
